note_html: Subjective: HPI: The patient, a 30-year-old male, presented with neck pain following a car accident one week ago. He described the pain as a 7 out of 10 in severity, with exacerbation upon movement of the neck. He has tried ibuprofen, which provided some relief, but the pain remains. He reports pain in the back of his neck, with some improvement with ibuprofen, but still a 5 out of 10. He denies any worsening of pain, but reports increased pain with neck movement. He has had headaches but none recently. He denies any hearing or visual disturbances. He has a history of playing football, but no previous neck issues. He did not seek medical attention after the accident due to cost concerns. He denies any swelling or bruising on his neck. Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED] Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED] Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED] Objective: Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED] Physical exam: The patient exhibited positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, with exacerbation upon flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending. The patient had some difficulty turning his neck from side to side. Test Results: The patient's x-ray results showed no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities. Assessment / Problem List: Assessment: The patient has neck strain, likely secondary to whiplash from the car accident. Problem list: Neck strain; Suspected whiplash; [NOT MENTIONED] Plan: I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for pain management. I also prescribed Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation. I advised the patient to avoid straining his neck and to be conservative with movement. I ordered an MRI to rule out any nerve impingement or other complications. I will refer the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor for further evaluation and treatment after the MRI results are available. Follow-up: I will review the MRI results and follow up with the patient to discuss further treatment options.
